zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Lambert於1489年6月12日出生在英國林可爾史爾吉爾頓時，他的父親Richard Franklin Lambert34歲，母親Lady Joyce Burton14歲。他於1512年與Joan Coney在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾吉爾頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1549年2月15日在英國英格蘭維爾爾迪斯波利享年59歲，埋葬在英國巴金哈姆爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Lambert于1489年6月12日出生在英国林可爾史爾吉爾頓时，他的父亲Richard Franklin Lambert34岁，母亲Lady Joyce Burton14岁。他于1512年与Joan Coney在英国英格兰林可爾史爾吉爾頓结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1549年2月15日在英国英格兰維爾爾迪斯波利享年59岁，埋葬在英国巴金哈姆爾。

nameMeanings.name: Lambert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, Walloon, Dutch, German, Polish, Czech, and Slovak: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements land ‘land, territory’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. In England, the native Old English form Landbeorht was replaced by Lambert, the Continental form of the name that was taken to England by the Normans from France. The name gained wider currency in Britain in the Middle Ages with the immigration of weavers from Flanders, among whom Saint Lambert or Lamprecht, bishop of Maastricht in around 700, was a popular cult figure. In Italy the name was popularized in the Middle Ages as a result of the fame of Lambert I and II, Dukes of Spoleto and Holy Roman Emperors. Lambert is the second most frequent surname in Wallonia. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Slovenian Lampret and Lampreht (see also Lampert ).

History: Aubin Lambert dit Champagne from Tourouvre in Orne, France, married Élisabeth Aubert in Quebec City, QC, in 1670; see also Champagne . Eustache Lambert from Boulogne-sur-Mer in Pas-de-Calais, France, married Marie Laurence in France c. 1656, died in Quebec City, QC, in 1673. Pierre Lambert from Fourmetot in Eure, France, married Marie Normand in Quebec City, QC, in 1680. Augustin Hébert dit Lambert, a descendant of Augustin Hébert from Paris, France, married Françoise Petit in Varennes, QC, in 1761. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Lambers
meanings: Dutch: patronymic from the personal name   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lamberth
meanings: English: variant of   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lambertus
meanings: German and Dutch: humanistic name, Latinized form of the personal name   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lamberti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Carlo, Gaetano, Luciano, Amedeo, Angelo, Biagio, Domenic, Donato, Enrico, Gino, Iolanda.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Lamberto, an equivalent of Lambert . This surname is also found in Corsica and southern France.
German and Dutch: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name   Lambert  , using the genitive of its Latinized form   Lambertus  .

variants.name: Lamberty
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Ana, Dimas, Jose, Juan, Altagracia, Angel, Blanca, Efrain, Fernando, Jaime, Ramon.
German and French: Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name Lambert , from Lamberti, the genitive of its Latinized form Lambertus . Compare Lamberti 2.

variants.name: Lambertz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Heinz, Erna, Johann, Ralf.
North German: patronymic from   Lambert  .

variants.name: Lamberto
meanings: Italian and Spanish: from the personal name Lamberto (see Lambert ).

variants.name: Lampert
meanings: English, North German, Dutch, and Slovak; Hungarian (Lampért): variant of Lambert . In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed some cognates from other languages, for example Slovenian Lampret and Lampreht.
English: variant of Lambard, an occupational name from Middle English lambherd, lambhird, lambhurd ‘lamb-herd’ (Old English lamb + hierde).

variants.name: Lambertsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik.
Dutch, Danish, North German, and Norwegian: patronymic from the personal name   Lambert  . This surname has died out in the Netherlands.
History: Cornelius Lambertsen came from Utrecht, the Netherlands, arriving at New Amsterdam in New Netherland (now New York City, NY) in 1642.

variants.name: Lamb
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Lamb, a pet form of Lambert .
English: nickname for a meek and inoffensive person, from Middle English lamb, or a metonymic occupational name for a keeper of lambs. See also Lamm .

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Luain ‘descendant of the warrior’, formerly Anglicized as O'Loan (see Lane 3). MacLysaght comments: "The form Lamb(e), which results from a more than usually absurd pseudo-translation (uan ‘lamb’), is now much more numerous than O'Loan itself.".

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Lambert was born on 12 June 1489, in Kirton, Lincolnshire, England, his father, Richard Franklin Lambert, was 34 and his mother, Lady Joyce Burton, was 14. He married Joan Coney in 1512, in Kirton, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 15 February 1549, in Tisbury, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 59, and was buried in Buckinghamshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Lambert est né le 12 juin 1489, à Kirton, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard Franklin Lambert, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Lady Joyce Burton, avait 14 ans. Il a épousé Joan Coney en 1512, à Kirton, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 15 février 1549, à Tisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 59 ans, et a été enterré à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Lambert è nato il il 12 giugno 1489 nel Kirton, Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, Richard Franklin Lambert, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Lady Joyce Burton, aveva 14 anni. Lui sposato Joan Coney nel 1512, nel Kirton, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 15 febbraio 1549, nel Tisbury, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 59 anni, e fu sepolto a Buckinghamshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Lambertが1489年6月12日にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアキルトンで生まれた時，父Richard Franklin Lambertは34歳，母Lady Joyce Burtonは14歳であった。1512年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアキルトンでJoan Coneyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1549年2月15日にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアティスブリーで59歳で死去し，イギリスブクインガムシュアイアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Lambert(이)가 1489년 6월 12일 영국 링코실르 커턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Franklin Lambert(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Joyce Burton(은)는 14세였다. 그는 1512년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 커턴에서 Joan Coney(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1549년 2월 15일 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 티스버이에서 59세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 벅잉함실르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Lambert nasceu em 12 de junho de 1489, em Kirton, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Franklin Lambert, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Lady Joyce Burton, tinha 14 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Coney em 1512, em Kirton, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 15 de fevereiro de 1549, em Tisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 59 anos, e foi sepultado em Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Lambert родился 12 июня 1489 г. в местности киртон, линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard Franklin Lambert было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Joyce Burton, 14. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Coney в 1512 г. в местности киртон, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 15 февраля 1549 г. в местности тисбури, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 59 лет и был похоронен в местности буккингхамшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Lambert nació el 12 de junio de 1489, en Kirton, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard Franklin Lambert, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Lady Joyce Burton, tenía 14 años. Se casó con Joan Coney en 1512, en Kirton, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 15 de febrero de 1549, en Tisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 59 años, y fue sepultado en Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra.
